title: lathyrus montanus var. divaricatus
intro: Lathyrus montanus var. divaricatus, also known as the Rocky Mountain sweet pea or sweet pea of the mountains, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The plant contains Lathyrine, a toxic amino acid that can be used medicinally in small quantities, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The medicinal properties of Lathyrus montanus var. divaricatus can provide relief from pain and inflammation, and it has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for use in herbal remedies, with the dried flowers and leaves being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
